purpose: Phase 6 launcher controller. The IIFE runs immediately at script-eval time (script lives at the end of body, so all buttons are already parsed). If ?random=1 is present in the URL, it picks a uniform-random variant (with ?autoloop=1&random=1 baked in), routes through applyTextColorParam to append the user's textcolor mode where appropriate, and navigates with location.replace so the launcher doesn't pollute history. Otherwise it wires click handlers on every .chamber button (intercepting the RANDOM sentinel), a document-level keydown handler that matches the button's data-key attribute (skipping when focus is in a textarea/input), the Display Mode UI (initDisplayMode), the Enrichment Center Credits UI (initEnrichmentCredits), the Override Track UI (initOverrideTrack), then calls focusLastVariant to give keyboard focus to the chamber the user last launched. navigate() saves the clicked target to localStorage.aperture.lastVariant and applies applyTextColorParam before location.assign. initDisplayMode hydrates 4 radios + a `<input type=color>` from localStorage.aperture.textColorMode + localStorage.aperture.customColor, autosaves on change, and toggles the picker's .hidden class based on CUSTOM selection. initEnrichmentCredits manages the .txt credit override (same as A3). initOverrideTrack manages the IndexedDB audio override (same as A4); WallpaperEngine mode is detected as either ?we=1 in the URL OR typeof window.wallpaperPropertyListener !== 'undefined' (runtime sniff for the WE-injected global) and greys out the section.
invariants: VARIANTS array holds the three navigable variant URLs (each ending in /index.html?autoloop=1&random=1, NOT a bare directory) so the random-pick navigation works across HTTP servers, file://, WSL file shares (\\wsl.localhost), and Tauri webview. pickRandom selects one with uniform probability.
The 'RANDOM' sentinel on a button's data-target triggers pickRandom; any other value is treated as a literal URL and passed to location.assign.

?random=1 on the launcher itself triggers location.replace (not assign) so the launcher entry is overwritten in history, leaving Back navigation on the credits page to return to the previous external referrer rather than bouncing back into the random picker.

Hotkey matching is case-insensitive (key is lowercased) and matched via [data-key] attribute selector; the canonical hotkeys are '1', '2', '3', 'r'. The keydown handler bails out when focus is in a TEXTAREA or INPUT so typing into the Enrichment Credits textarea cannot trigger a chamber jump.

localStorage keys are 'aperture.customCredits' (string), 'aperture.creditsMode' (loop|stop), 'aperture.textColorMode' (default|cycle|rainbow|custom), 'aperture.customColor' (6-digit hex like #ff00ff), and 'aperture.lastVariant' (full chamber data-target URL). The credits keys are read by every variant cake.js via applyCustomCredits during DOMContentLoaded; the textColor keys feed applyTextColorParam at navigation time; lastVariant is read on launcher load by focusLastVariant.
MAX_CHARS=20000 is enforced both via <textarea maxlength> (silent browser cap) and via FileReader slice; both paths show the truncation notice for 5 seconds.

LOOP_THRESHOLD=2500 — under this length the MODE fieldset is visible and the variant uses naturalCreditsDelay; at-or-over this length the fieldset is hidden and the variant falls through to the STRETCH math regardless of the saved mode.

IndexedDB schema is shared with variant cake.js files: database 'aperture' version 1, object store 'audio' (no keyPath), key 'customAudio' holding { blob, filename, durationSec }. The upgrade handler in idbOpen creates the store if missing — both launcher and variants must use this exact shape.

initOverrideTrack WE-mode branch returns BEFORE wiring any event handlers, so the LOAD/RESET buttons truly no-op in WallpaperEngine mode (not just a CSS dim). WE mode is true when either (a) ?we=1 is in the URL OR (b) window.wallpaperPropertyListener is defined at script-eval time (WE injects this global; URL param is the canonical signal for tests).

supportsTextColor(url) is the gatekeeper for applyTextColorParam — it returns true for URLs containing 'portal/' or 'portal2/portal1style/' but false for 'portal2/' (gold theme intentionally untouched). Order of substring checks matters: 'portal2/portal1style/' must be tested BEFORE 'portal2/' since the former contains the latter as a substring.

applyTextColorParam appends '&textcolor=<value>' (or '?textcolor=<value>' if the URL has no query string yet). For custom mode, the hex is URL-encoded via encodeURIComponent so '#ff00ff' becomes '%23ff00ff'; variant cake.js URLSearchParams.get() decodes back. Invalid hex (regex fail) silently no-ops — URL is returned unchanged.

focusLastVariant runs AFTER initDisplayMode + initEnrichmentCredits + initOverrideTrack so user-visible UI is fully rendered before keyboard focus is moved. Calling btn.focus() with no last-variant saved is a no-op (early return).

The audio file picker uses a transient <audio> probe to read duration on the client side before writing to IDB — this is the only way to know cake.creditsMaxTime ahead of time. The probe is created with URL.createObjectURL, listens for loadedmetadata or error, and the URL is revoked via URL.revokeObjectURL once finished (or after a 10s timeout) to avoid blob URL leaks.
gotchas: The keydown handler calls preventDefault when the key matches a button. Hotkeys that collide with browser shortcuts (none of 1/2/3/R do by default) would suppress those shortcuts on this page only.
If the launcher is hit at random=1 with no JS, the random redirect never happens and the picker is shown — graceful degradation but no automated fallback.
VARIANTS URLs are relative (../portal/, ../portal2/, ../portal2/portal1style/); they assume launcher/ sits as a sibling of those variant folders. Move the launcher and these break.

The script intentionally does NOT wait for DOMContentLoaded; it relies on the <script> tag being at end of <body> so .chamber buttons are already in the DOM. Moving the tag into <head> without defer breaks button binding.

All localStorage access goes through safeGet/safeSet/safeRemove which swallow exceptions — Safari private mode and storage-quota errors degrade gracefully (custom credits don't persist but UI still functions).

All IndexedDB access goes through idbOpen/idbPutAudio/idbGetAudio/idbDeleteAudio which swallow exceptions — Firefox private mode and quota errors invoke the callback with null/false and the UI shows an inline error pill rather than crashing.

Paste-truncation detection uses event.inputType === 'insertFromPaste' + textarea.value.length === MAX_CHARS; this is best-effort and won't fire on legacy browsers that don't expose inputType on InputEvent.
picker.value = '' is called after each load so re-selecting the same file fires a change event again.

audioPicker.value = '' is set IMMEDIATELY in the change handler (before processing) so re-selecting the same file fires the change event again. If moved after the IDB put, slow IDB writes would block re-selection briefly.

The 10s safety timeout on the audio probe is a coarse fallback for cases where loadedmetadata neither fires nor errors (very rare). Without it, a hung probe holds a blob URL until the next page navigation.
Override Track ?we=1 detection uses the same URLSearchParams instance (params) shared with the random-redirect check at the top of the IIFE — keeps the param access cheap and consistent.

CREDITS_KEY / MODE_KEY / TEXTCOLOR_KEY / CUSTOM_COLOR_KEY / LAST_VARIANT_KEY / MAX_CHARS / LOOP_THRESHOLD constants must be declared and assigned BEFORE the `params = new URLSearchParams(...)` block — both the ?random=1 redirect (which calls applyTextColorParam(pickRandom())) and the else-branch init calls reference them via safeGet. var hoists the binding but not the value, so reordering them below would make safeGet(undefined) return null silently, breaking hydration on first paint.

supportsTextColor uses substring matching; ANY URL containing 'portal2/portal1style/' returns true (so the textcolor param IS appended). The order matters because 'portal2/portal1style/' contains 'portal2/' as a substring — the longer check runs first.

Initialization order in DOMContentLoaded matters for A6 focus: focusLastVariant() is the LAST call in the else branch so it runs after every chamber button is wired. If a future feature calls .focus() somewhere earlier (e.g. an autofocus on a textarea), focusLastVariant's focus call wins because it runs last.
